mnemonics: ICE-CUBES: Identify problem, Choose design, Evaluate efficiency, Code, Understand correctness, Benchmark, Examine, Solve
keyPoints: An algorithm is a sequence of unambiguous instructions for solving a problem.
Algorithm design techniques include brute force, divide and conquer, decrease and conquer, transform and conquer, greedy approach, dynamic programming, and backtracking.
Algorithm analysis focuses on time efficiency (how fast it runs) and space efficiency (how much memory it uses).
commonMistakes: Confusing worst-case, average-case, and best-case time complexity analyses.
Focusing solely on time efficiency while neglecting space efficiency considerations.
Assuming a correct algorithm without a formal proof of correctness.
